title: medicago hispida var. apiculata
intro: Medicago hispida var. apiculata, also known as the hairy medick, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family and is a variant of the common medick (Medicago hispida). The hairy medick has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Medicago hispida var. apiculata include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic and digestive aid. Herbal preparations of hairy medick include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made by steeping the dried plant material in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
